zh.lifeSummary: 當John Richard Barker於1488年出生在英國越可爾約克時，他的父親Rev John Barker26歲，母親Margaret Coleclough26歲。他和Dame Elizabeth Hill至少有1個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1572年5月4日在英國英格蘭素福爾可易普斯維其享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾聶德林頓。

nameMeanings.name: Barker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a tanner of leather, from Middle English barkere ‘tanner’, tree bark having been used as the tanning agent.

English: occupational name for a shepherd, from Middle English berker, bercher (Old French berchier, bercher, berkier, berker, Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’, genitive berbicis). With the change of -ar- to -er- in Middle English, this became indistinguishable from the preceding name (see 1 above).
Americanized form of German   Berger   or   Barger  .

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When John Richard Barker was born in 1488, in York, Yorkshire, England, his father, Rev John Barker, was 26 and his mother, Margaret Coleclough, was 26. He had at least 1 son and 4 daughters with Dame Elizabeth Hill. He died on 4 May 1572, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84, and was buried in Knedlington, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles avec Dame Elizabeth Hill. Il est décédé le 4 mai 1572, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à Knedlington, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Richard Barker è nato il nel 1488 nel York, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Rev John Barker, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Coleclough, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie con Dame Elizabeth Hill. Morì il 4 maggio 1572, nel Ipswich, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolto a Knedlington, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Richard Barkerが1488年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヨークで生まれた時，父Rev John Barkerは26歳，母Margaret Colecloughは26歳であった。彼はDame Elizabeth Hillとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1572年5月4日にイギリスイギリススフォルクイプスウィチで84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアネドリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Richard Barker(이)가 1488년 영국 요르크실르 요르크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rev John Barker(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Coleclough(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Dame Elizabeth Hill(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1572년 5월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 네들링턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Richard Barker родился в 1488 г. в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Rev John Barker было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Coleclough, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери от Dame Elizabeth Hill. Он умер 4 мая 1572 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности недлингтон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
